message.content: Adversaries may exploit SID-History Injection to escalate privileges and bypass access controls by manipulating the Windows security identifier (SID) history attribute. This technique involves inserting additional SIDs into the SID-History attribute of an account, which can then be used to impersonate high-privilege users or groups, such as Enterprise Administrators. This manipulation allows adversaries to gain elevated access to local resources and perform lateral movements across domains using methods like Remote Services, SMB/Windows Admin Shares, or Windows Remote Management. Tools like Empire and Mimikatz facilitate this by adding or appending SIDs to a user's SID-History, enabling broader access and control, including the generation of Kerberos Golden Tickets and DCSync operations. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and auditing changes to SID-History attributes, restricting Domain Administrator privileges, and employing robust access control measures to detect and prevent unauthorized SID manipulations.
